# UC-22.2.13 · NIS2 Asset Management and Configuration Baseline

## Description

Uses fifteen-minute (or better) summarized east-west traffic from the accelerated Network Traffic model and compares each internal `src` IP against the ES asset lookup to expose high-volume senders that lack an approved CMDB category.

## Value

NIS2 Article 21 expects inventories that stay trustworthy between annual audits; continuously proving that mystery IPs do not dominate traffic gives assurance that change windows, segmentation projects, and CMDB hygiene actually stick—not just spreadsheets.

## Implementation

(1) Keep `asset_lookup_by_str` synced from ServiceNow `cmdb_ci_server` nightly. (2) Accelerate CIM Network_Traffic for 30+ days. (3) Run search daily; route top `/16` hits to network engineering. (4) Join `uc-22.2.9` dashboard KPIs. (5) Reference `nis2_asset_exception_register.csv` for lab VLANs.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) ≥7.2 with **Configure → Data Enrichment → Asset and Identity** imports enabled; schedule `identity` merge hourly and `asset` nightly.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) pulling `cmdb_ci_server` via REST `/api/now/table/cmdb_ci_server?sysparm_query=sys_class_name=cmdb_ci_linux_server` or equivalent filter; map to `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server` with fields `ip_address`, `host_name`, `managed_by`, `u_nis2_tier`.
• CIM Network_Traffic acceleration covering at least 14 days of east-west NetFlow or Zeek `conn` logs re-tagged into `All_Traffic` datasets.
• Disk budget: summary buckets ~200 MB/day per 10k flows/sec after filtering.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **Asset import**: under ES **Asset Management**, configure prioritized list ServiceNow > Qualys > Active Directory; ensure `asset_lookup_by_str` contains RFC1918 keys.
• **Flow ingestion**: confirm `props.conf` sets `EVAL-bytes = bytes_in + bytes_out` for bi-directional flows.
• ServiceNow TA: enable modular input with poll 900s, OAuth stored in **Passwords**.
• Run baseline `| inputlookup asset_lookup_by_str | stats count` — should be ≥95% of routed IPs for critical VLANs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use provided `spl`. Add post-process:
```spl
| lookup nis2_asset_exception_register src OUTPUT reason
| where isnull(reason)
```
Save correlation search **UC-22.2.13** firing when `total_bytes > 50e9`.

Step 3 — Validate
• Select an unknown `src` and trace ARP table / VMware port group to confirm it is genuinely orphan vs stale CMDB row.
• Compare `total_bytes` totals to NetFlow collector UI (e.g., **Arista CloudVision** or **Cisco DNA Traffic Analytics**) for same hour.
• Validate ServiceNow row: `index=cmdb host_name=* | search <candidate>` must return `sys_id`.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• ES Incident Review panel + executive posture dashboard row "Unknown asset GB/day".
• Weekly ticket to CMDB steward with CSV signed via `evidenceSigning`.
• Integrate Qualys add-on ([Splunkbase 2964](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964)) for vulnerability context join on IP.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **tstats empty** — Network_Traffic not accelerated; enable in **Settings**.
• **Everything unknown** — asset merge not running; check `splunkd.log` for `AssetExtraction` errors.
• **All results guest Wi-Fi** — missing `cidrmatch` filter; add to base search.
• **False positives after SD-WAN migration** — refresh `asset_lookup_by_str` import mappings.


## SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports, sum(All_Traffic.bytes) as total_bytes
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.src
| rename All_Traffic.src as src
| lookup asset_lookup_by_str key AS src OUTPUT category, priority, owner, nt_host
| where isnull(category) OR category="unknown"
| sort - total_bytes
| head 100
| table src, nt_host, category, owner, ports, total_bytes
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table of unknown senders, choropleth if geo IP enabled, link to Qualys host detail.
